rulings.Case Title: GLENN MENSING VS. LYNDON LIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Further Answer Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction And Other Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 6, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF GLENN MENSING'S Motion To Compel Defendants Further Answer Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction And Other Sanctions. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED without prejudice. A notice of motion for order compelling answers must be accompanied by a separate document setting forth the following information: each interrogatory (or other discovery request) to which further answer is sought, numbered and set forth verbatim; the answer or objection made by the opposing party to each such discovery request, also verbatim; the reason why further responses should be ordered by the court (i.e., the factual or legal reason why the objection is invalid or the answer given is incomplete); if necessary, the text of all definitions, instructions and other matters required to understand each discovery request and the response to it; other discovery requests and responses if they are relevant to why further responses are necessary to the present discovery request; and a summary of any pleadings or other documents on file by the party relying on them in the present discovery dispute. CRC 3.1345(c). Here, no separate statement was filed and served with the Motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THE MCCAFFREY GROUP INC. et al VS. COOLEY LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cooley Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 6, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT COOLEY LLP'S Motion To Compel Discovery. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED and DENIED in part, as follows. This ruling applies to Cooley's First and Second Set of Form Interrogatories. Form Interrogatory 4.1: GRANTED. The interrogatory requests information for "any policy if insurance through which you were or might be insured¿" Granite's payment of a settlement is evidence that plaintiff was or might have been insured. Form Interrogatory 9.1: DENIED. Plaintiff has provided a code-complaint response. The response states "[a]ll plaintiffs seek the following damages¿" Form Interrogatory 17.1: GRANTED. Requests for Production of Documents #1, 4, 5-8 (to TMG): GRANTED. The Court notes that the collateral source rules does not prevent Cooley from seeking discovery regarding payments by Granite. Defendant shall provide amended responses and produce responsive documents within 21 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN TIN DDS et al VS. PENINSULA FIRE PROTECTION, INC, A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday May 6, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JUSTIN TIN, SAN BRUNO AVENUE DENTAL CLINIC, LLC, SAN BRUNO PROPERTIES LLC'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED, as follows. Defendant's Objection that for the Motion to be meritorious it must be titled a motion to compel further responses is overruled for two reasons. First, the MPA cites to CCP Sections 2031.010 and 2031.300 which sections include both a motion to compel and motion to compel further responses. Second, defendant failed to respond at all to RFPs 3-7. A motion to compel these responses would be titled a motion to compel responses. Defendant's objection that the motion was brought on behalf of two additional parties is noted, but insufficient to deny the Motion. The Motion is GRANTED as to RFP 1-10. Defendant Greatwall shall provide amended responses and produce responsive documents within 21 days of this order, together with a privilege log listing all documents withheld on the basis of privilege. Sanctions in the amount of $600 are awarded against Defendant Greatwall Building Contractor, Inc. and its attorney Henry Wirta. Sanctions are payable within 21 days of this order. Defendant's objection that plaintiffs' counsel failed to provide foundational facts to support the sanctions award is overruled. See Catalano Decl., 3:7-9. Defendant has not presented any evidence that defendant opposed the motion "with substantial justification" or other circumstances make sanctions "unjust." CCP §§ 2031.320(b) (document production statute), 2023.030(a) (general sanctions statute). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SANTOS RAFAEL PUC MAY ET AL VS. ADIB KHOURI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Under Ccp Section 473(B)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 6, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF SANTOS PUC MAY, JOSE DELFINO PUC Motion Under CCP Section 473(B). Denied. The mandatory attorney fault provision of CCP 473(b) only applies to "default," "default judgment," and "dismissals." Relief from an order sustaining a demurrer without leave to amend is therefore not available under this subsection. (English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 130.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JOHN S EDWARDS VS. ARIEL D EDWARDS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 6, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT ARIEL EDWARDS Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint. Denied. The motion does not satisfy the first prong of CCP 425.16 that plaintiff's causes of action are based on petitioning activity. The gravamen of plaintiff's claims is that the plaintiff was tricked out of his property. Plaintiff is not seeking damages based on the reports to CPS. The fact that protected activity may be lurking in the background of the parties' dispute does not transform this property dispute case into a SLAPP suit. (In re Episcopal Church Cases (2009) 45 Cal.4th 467, 477-78; Renewable Resources Coalition, Inc. v. Pebble Mines (2013) 218 Cal. App. 4th 384 ). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(HK/302)
